questions.question: What is the main goal of developing a MapReduce program to find the tags associated with each movie by analyzing movie lens data?
options: B) To find the average rating of a movie
C) To identify the tags associated with each movie
D) To calculate the standard deviation of ratings
questions.explanation: The main goal is to identify the tags associated with each movie by analyzing the movie lens data.

questions.question: Which Spark feature is used to process large datasets in parallel?
questions.explanation: All of the above (RDD, DataFrame, Dataset) are used to process large datasets in parallel.

questions.question: What type of data is typically stored in a MovieLens dataset?
options: A) User ratings and movie metadata
B) User profiles and movie genres
C) Movie ratings and user demographics
D) User reviews and movie summaries
questions.explanation: User ratings and movie metadata are typically stored in a MovieLens dataset.

questions.question: What is a common technique used in MapReduce to handle missing values?
options: A) Fill missing values with the mean
B) Fill missing values with the median
questions.explanation: Fill missing values with the median is a common technique used in MapReduce to handle missing values.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of using a MapReduce program to analyze MovieLens data?
questions.explanation: All of the above (faster data processing, improved data quality, increased scalability) are primary benefits of using a MapReduce program to analyze MovieLens data.
